text:
There's a grove like no other. It’s hidden in the dense forest behind an old monastery. There, under moonlit nights, a peculiar sight takes place. Every night, from late summer till first frost, owls perform aerial dances around an ancient oak.

These owls aren’t just any owls—each has a vibrant plumage of purples and blues, eyes that glow ethereal under the moonlight. They communicate through beautifulsongs, melodic echoes that drift through the trees like soft whispers.

Every dance follows a rhythm ancient, a story told through movement alone. Sometimes they’re paired together in duets, other times solo. At dusk, they gather around their favourite branch, a sort of assembly point. There, they sing their evening songs together, creating sounds that weave through the forest.

One night, a young owl, adventurous and curious, climbed up to perch on the oldest branch. It listened intently, its wings folding around it comfortably. The owl didn
